compiled_state.title: As a developer with a human designer, I can opt into Figma collaboration so that an importable SVG is generated
compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart
compiled_state.description:
## Description

**What**: When the design.figma_collaboration config flag is true for a design, the design-wireframe skill generates a Figma-import-optimized wireframe.svg alongside the standard 3-artifact manifest, creates the designs/{uuid}/ directory, and tags the story design:awaiting_import with instructions for the user to import the SVG into Figma.
**Why**: Enables human designers to review and revise DSO's proposed designs in their native tool (Figma) by providing an importable artifact.
**Scope**:
- IN: Figma-optimized SVG structure (semantic <g id="..."> group names matching spatial-layout.json component IDs, inline styles, valid SVG 1.1), designs/{uuid}/ directory creation, .gitignore entry for designs/**/*.png, design:awaiting_import tag with user instructions, design.figma_collaboration config key (default: false)
- OUT: Approval workflow (S2), sprint filtering (S3), implementation agent changes (S4)

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, setting design.figma_collaboration=true in dso-config.conf causes design-wireframe to generate a wireframe.svg with semantic <g> group names whose IDs match spatial-layout.json component IDs, inline styles, and valid SVG 1.1 structure importable by Figma
  ← Satisfies: "wireframe.svg is written with Figma-optimized structure"
- When this story is complete, the designs/{uuid}/ directory is created when the SVG is written, with a .gitignore entry excluding *.png files from version control
  ← Satisfies: "writes it to the design artifacts directory"
- When this story is complete, the story is tagged design:awaiting_import with a ticket comment containing instructions: "Import designs/{uuid}/wireframe.svg into Figma. After the designer revises, export a PNG of the revised design to designs/{uuid}/figma-revision.png and run the approval command."
  ← Satisfies: "story is tagged design:awaiting_import with instructions"
- When this story is complete, projects without design.figma_collaboration=true are completely unaffected — no new artifacts, no tags, no directory creation
  ← Satisfies: "No effect on projects without the flag"
- Unit tests written and passing for all new or modified logic

## Considerations

- [Testing] SVG ID-linkage validation: Figma-optimized IDs must pass the existing design-wireframe ID-linkage validation checkpoint (JSON_IDS ⊆ SVG_IDS, SVG_IDS ⊆ JSON_IDS)
- [Reliability] designs/ directory created atomically with SVG write; .gitignore prevents PNG bloat in VCS
- [Maintainability] Tag constants (design:awaiting_import, design:approved) should be defined in a way that S2 and S3 can consume without hardcoding
- [Integration] S1 modifies SVG output of design-wireframe. Figma-specific IDs must be additive to the existing ID-linkage validation checkpoint — must not break JSON/SVG/Token ID consistency check

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue — one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.
